2605.21227.title: Do LLMs Know What Luxembourgish Borrows? Probing Lexical Neology in Low-Resource Multilingual Models
2605.21227.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for writing assistance in small contact languages, yet it is unclear whether they respect community norms around lexical borrowing and neology. We introduce LexNeo-Bench, a 3{,}050-instance token-level benchmark derived from LuxBorrow, a large-scale Luxembourgish news corpus, where target tokens are labelled as native or as French, German, or English borrowings. Using this benchmark, we probe three multilingual LLMs across 34 prompt settings on two tasks: borrowing type classification and a binary lexical-innovation proxy (borrowing versus native). Without external context, models perform only slightly above chance on borrowing classification, so we construct a linguistic knowledge graph that encodes donor language, morphological patterns, and lexical analogues, and inject instance-specific subgraphs into the prompt. Knowledge-graph prompts raise borrowing classification accuracy from 25 -- 35\% up to 71 -- 81\% and largely close the gap between small and large models, while leaving neology detection difficult and sensitive to few-shot design. Our results show that lexicon-aware prompting is highly beneficial for robust borrowing judgments in low-resource contact languages and that lexical resources can serve as structured context for LLM evaluation. This study was carried out within the ENEOLI COST Action and examines borrowing as a form of lexical innovation in multilingual Luxembourgish data.
2605.21227.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地用於小型接觸語言的寫作輔助，但尚不清楚它們是否遵循社區在詞彙借用和新詞創造方面的規範。<lb/>我們介紹了 LexNeo-Bench，這是一個由 LuxBorrow 衍生的 3{,}050 個實例的標記級基準，LuxBorrow 是一個大規模的盧森堡新聞語料庫，其中目標標記被標記為本土或法語、德語或英語的借用詞。<lb/>利用這個基準，我們在兩個任務上對三個多語言 LLM 進行了 34 種提示設置的探測：借用類型分類和二元詞彙創新代理（借用與本土）。<lb/>在沒有外部上下文的情況下，模型在借用分類上的表現僅略高於隨機，因此我們構建了一個語言知識圖，編碼捐贈語言、形態模式和詞彙類比，並將實例特定的子圖注入提示中。<lb/>知識圖提示將借用分類的準確率從 25 -- 35\% 提高到 71 -- 81\%，並在很大程度上縮小了小型和大型模型之間的差距，同時使新詞檢測變得困難且對少量樣本設計敏感。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，詞彙感知提示對於在資源匱乏的接觸語言中進行穩健的借用判斷非常有利，並且詞彙資源可以作為 LLM 評估的結構化上下文。<lb/>這項研究是在 ENEOLI COST 行動內進行的，並檢視了借用作為多語言盧森堡數據中詞彙創新的一種形式。
2605.21227.author: Nina Hosseini-Kivanani et.al.
